Q3 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

In a bolt with the shank turned down to the core diameter of the thread, which of the following statements is most accurate regarding its mechanical behaviour?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
If the shaft of the bolt is turned down to a diameter equal to or even slightly less than the core diameter of the thread (Dc), then shank of the bolt will undergo a higher stress. This means that a shaft will absorb a large portion of the energy, thus relieving the material at the sections near the thread. The bolt in this way, becomes stronger and lighter and it increase the shock absorbing capacity of the bolt because of an increased modulus of resilience. This gives us bolts of uniform
strength.

Q6 Not checked

Not attempted

A 45 mm diameter solid shaft is welded to a flat plate by 12 mm fillet weld as shown in the figure. If the maximum shear intensity in the weld material is not to exceed 75 MPa, the maximum torque that the weld can sustain is _____ kN-m. (Correct upto three decimal places)

 
 
NAT
Correct answer: 2.023
Solution

2.023 (2.0 - 2.1)

 
 
Q7 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A shaft rotating at constant speed is subjected to variable load. The ball bearings supporting shaft are subjected to stationary equivalent radial load of 3 kN for 20% of time 2 kN for 30% time 1 kN for remaining time of cycle. The equivalent radial load will be _____ kN. (Correct upto 3 decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 2.025
Solution

2.025 (2.0 - 2.2)

 
Q8 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

In a steam engine the maximum steam pressure is 1.5 N/mm2 absolute and the back pressure is 0.5 N/mm2 absolute. The cylinder diameter is 0.3 m. If allowable stress in mild steel is 45 N/mm2 in tension. The diameter of the screwed end of the piston rod will be _____ mm. (Correct upto two decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 44.72
Solution

44.72 (44 - 45)

 
Q9 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements about the Lewis form factor is correct?

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, b)
Some important points about Lewis form factor are
• It is used in Lewis equation to compute bending stress in gear tooth.
• It is dimensionless and purely geometric.
• Depends on number of teeth, pressure angle, addendum.
• It is independent of module.

Q15 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A shaft made of mild steel is required to transmit 90 kW at 280 rpm. The supported length of the shaft is 3 meters. It carries two pulleys each weighing 1600 N supported at a distance of 1 metre from the ends respectively. Assuming allowable shear stress to be 60 MPa. The diameter of the shaft is ______ mm. (Rounded off to next integer)

NAT
Correct answer: 67
Solution

67 (66 - 68)

 
 
Q16 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A Bronze spur pinion rotating at 650 rpm drives a cast iron spur gear at a transmission ratio of 4:1. The allowable static stresses for the bronze pinion and cast iron gear are 85 MPa and 105 MPa respectively. The pinion has 18 standard 20° full depth involute teeth of module 8 mm. The face width of both the gears is 85 mm. The power that can be transmitted from the stand point of
strength is ______ kW. (Correct upto two decimal places) [Assume Lewis form factor for gear and pinion to be 0.44 and 0.31 respectively]

NAT
Correct answer: 33.36
Solution

33.36 (32 - 35)

Correct answer: N/A

(a, b)
